questions.question: What does 'opening' refer to in morphological image processing?
options: A) The process of filling or completing shapes in an image
B) The process of shrinking or reducing shapes in an image
C) The process of converting images to grayscale
D) The process of applying noise reduction techniques
questions.explanation: Opening in morphological image processing refers to the process of shrinking or reducing shapes in an image by applying an erosion operation followed by a dilation operation.

questions.question: What is the difference between Erosion and Dilation in morphological image processing?
options: A) Erosion reduces noise and Dilation reduces shape
B) Erosion expands shapes and Dilation reduces shapes
C) Erosion shrinks shapes and Dilation fills shapes
D) Erosion closes shapes and Dilation opens shapes
questions.explanation: Erosion is a process that shrinks shapes, while dilation is a process that fills shapes, making option C) Erosion shrinks shapes and Dilation fills shapes the correct answer.

questions.question: What is the effect of applying an 'Opening' operation to an image?
options: A) It increases the contrast of the image
B) It reduces noise in the image
C) It changes the color palette of the image
D) It reduces the resolution of the image
questions.explanation: Applying an 'Opening' operation to an image reduces noise in the image by shrinking or reducing shapes.

questions.question: What is the effect of applying a 'Closing' operation to an image?
options: A) It increases the contrast of the image
B) It reduces noise in the image
C) It changes the color palette of the image
D) It removes noise and fills gaps in the image
questions.explanation: Applying a 'Closing' operation to an image removes noise and fills gaps in the image by expanding or growing shapes.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Hit-or-Miss morphological operation?
options: A) To connect disjointed objects in an image
B) To separate objects in an image
C) To identify major features in an image
D) To enhance image details
questions.explanation: The Hit-or-Miss operation is used to identify major features in an image by detecting areas that match a specific shape or pattern.
